feature: Bottom-center "Return to base" button shown on mission complete; taps engageAutopilot() to autopilot home and dock.
critic_findings: DECISIONS §42 touch bug: bare click handler dead while steering (second-thumb tap suppressed) — resolved via split touchstart/mouse wiring
planned_tests: existing client suite only (no new test file); i18n JSON parse check — plan rationale: DOM/CSS/i18n-only affordance, no new runtime logic

feature: Grab (tractor) rebalanced to an inverse-square pull field (field=strength*5/dist^2, engaged where field>=0.4); range is emergent + weight-independent (base ~11.2u, Advanced ~15.8u = sqrt(2)x base); strength values kept 10/20; shop shows raw strength relabeled as abstract grab strength.
critic_findings: pullSpeed(10,10,3) anchor written 500/9 must be 50/9 — would ship a failing test
reviewer_findings: stale 'range = strength' claim in SUMMARY.md:1553 test-inventory (blocking)
original Grab DECISIONS entry lacked a superseded-by-§57 pointer (minor)
planned_tests: drops.test.js distance-aware pullSpeed anchors (50/9 @d3, 2.0 @d5, closer=faster)
field() cutoff boundary crosses FIELD_CUTOFF exactly at range()
range() base sqrt(125), advanced sqrt(250), ratio===Math.SQRT2
zero/undefined weight falls back to WEIGHT_FALLBACK
server.test.js grab-seed strength 10/20 unchanged (SQLite+Postgres)

escaped_defects: reel-in speed felt too fast (reach was good) — not caught by automated tests; fixed same-day by the 2026-07-07 PULL_SPEED_SCALE=0.67 follow-up commit

feature: Distant recorded ghost-battle landmark at a fixed world point, shown in all missions except the freighter escort; player flies freely (fixed-offset re-center), births/deaths, ?dev in-game recorder + tuning panel, tier-gated.

BLOCKING: recorder acc:1e9 defeated the 20fps decimation → ~900 frames stamped 20fps → 45s playback at 1/3 speed
multiple APPROVE-first-pass re-verifies on maintainer-driven redesigns (panel, births, slot-0, fly-free/absolute-anchor)

sampleShip lerp + shortest-arc yaw + end-clamp
recenterAndQuantize fixed-offset + born-late
shape + bounded-formation guards over committed track

edits: re-anchor: slot-0 then fixed mean-offset (player flies)
absolute world anchor + gate flip to all-except-freighter

perf_gate: inactive (feature self-skips under ?bench; cost is draw-only, <=8 ships + <=24 bullets on High)

escaped_defects: ghosts invisible (readability treatment over-dimmed to nothing)
whole formation jumps downward at every birth/death (cast-centroid re-center)
2-4s dead-tail lag before the loop restarts (recording tail trimmed)

flags: planner: 8 revisions — but maintainer-driven VISUAL iteration discoverable only by playtest, not a discovery miss
live-test caught 4 defects the automated suite structurally cannot (all visual/feel)

feature: Gentle intro Level 0 (3 basic pirates one-at-a-time + rocket finale, enemyTotal 4) as the new first campaign level via a seed content-shift + current_progress+1 migration; new players auto-launch straight into the fight (no welcome/Take-off). Step 1 of the Level-0 cutscene effort.
critic_findings: BLOCKING: the plan omitted the briefing-chain test (server.test.js:158-197) which the level shift breaks — the first advance is now the intro (no briefing) so the whole briefing/reward chain moves down one advance; plan must restructure the test
verified the migration guards (SQLite user_version once-only; Postgres migrations_pg ledger + ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING RETURNING) prevent a catastrophic re-increment on every deploy
planned_tests: enemy_total EXPECTED shifted {level-1:4,...,level-5:22}
server.test progress-chain→level-5 + reset→level-5
briefing-chain restructured (leading no-briefing advance, MG/drone/unlockShop each one advance later)
per-level content shifted
visual 18-briefing-staged-reveal + 97-briefing-showcase landOn+1

edits: AUTO-LAUNCH: first launch drops straight into the fight — no welcome screen / Take-off (reversed the plan’s “intro lands on welcome, no client change”)

flags: planner: 2 revisions — both maintainer-driven at the review gate (briefing + auto-launch). ONBOARDING/LANDING-FLOW decisions (welcome screen, briefing, auto-launch, ship-select) were bundled as a discovery default (Q5) instead of surfaced to the maintainer — they had strong opinions only revealed when asked

feature: Two new-player intro fixes: (A) live combat on Take-off after the intro cutscene (finishIntro tears down the playback/cutscene session so animate() returns to the live update(dt) branch), extracted into a unit-tested makeReplaySession(); (B) reset-progress now replays the intro via a server-authoritative shouldPlayIntro() gate, dropping the client localStorage introSeen guard (DECISIONS 63).
critic_findings: non-blocking: Fix A self-check grep wording included cutDone/cutReturning which are retained field names (fixed the check)
planned_tests: client: makeReplaySession teardown-completeness (active toggles, deepEqual all 8 fields reset)
client: shouldPlayIntro gate incl. ('',true)===true reset->cutscene guard + headless opt-outs
server: served level descriptor.introTrace truthy at progress 1, falsy at 2, restored after reset

edits: maintainer required a regression test -> plan revised to extract a testable makeReplaySession() seam + unit test

escaped_defects: reset-progress did not replay the intro (client localStorage introSeen persisted across the server reset) — folded in as Fix B in the same branch

flags: plannerRevisions>1: revision 1 (testability) was a genuine planner+critic miss; revision 2 (Fix B) was maintainer-driven scope added during live-test, not a planner miss
live-test surfaced an escaped defect the automated suites could not catch (reset->intro), fixed as Fix B
perf gate: first run REGRESSION was machine-load noise (local server + npm install running); clean re-run FLAT

feature: EN/RU language selector added to the Settings modal + intro cutscene, reusing the existing setLanguage() i18n path (live, no reload) via a shared toggle-host registry
critic_findings: initial-load highlight would stay stuck on EN for any non-en resolved language (the exact RU-on-itch target user) because the host-rebuild loop lived only in setLanguage(), which bootstrap never calls — moved into applyTranslations()
the test plan did not guard that initial-load bug (scenario 14 started in EN, so it passed even with the bug) — added scenario 21-language-initial-ru
reviewer_findings: SUMMARY.md claimed the settings modal fits 'without internal scroll on a 1280x800 viewport', but the new Language row pushed internal overflow to 77px (was 19px) — doc-accuracy fix
planned_tests: langButtons() unit test in i18n.test.js
new visual scenario 21-language-initial-ru (RU active on first paint, guards the initial-load fix)
extend scenario 14-reset-progress (live switch updates all mounted hosts + chrome, modal still fits)
flags: code-reviewer agent missed a UI overlap (the new intro cutscene toggle painted on top of the always-visible settings gear — both top-left corner) despite being explicitly asked to check UI position vs existing elements; caught at the human-review gate and fixed (left:56px) before commit. reviewRounds counter (=1) did not trip, but this is the substantive reviewer miss of the run
critic earned its keep: caught a real initial-load regression (toggles stuck on EN for the RU-on-itch target user) before implementation
perf gate FLAT (CPU-only, js.total 1.80->1.68ms); the A/B run was slow (~17min) on this machine but completed clean
